content:
4 min read
Just now

--

git cherry-pick copies one or more specific commits from one branch and applies them to another branch without merging the entire branch.
It is commonly used to move bug fixes, hotfixes, or small changes between branches.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Git cherry-pickWhy Do We Need Cherry-Pick?
Suppose you have two branches.
master
A ----- Bfeature-2
A ----- B ----- C ----- D ----- E
Where:

• C → New feature

• D → Critical bug fix

• E → Another feature
The bug fix (D) must go to production immediately.
However, the feature branch is not ready.
If You Merge
git merge feature-2Result
master
A ----- B ------------------ M
\ /
C ----- D ----- EEverything gets merged:

• ✅ Bug fix

• ❌ Unfinished features
Not what we want.
Cherry-Pick Solution
Instead, copy only the required commit.
git cherry-pick DResult
master
A ----- B ----- D'feature-2
A ----- B ----- C ----- D ----- E
Notice:

• Only the bug fix is copied.

• Feature commits remain in the feature branch.
Step 1 — Find the Commit
Display commit history.
git log --onelineExample
a82f91f Add new feature file
c38b102 Fix typo in m1.txt
71fa820 Add another featureCopy the commit hash you want.
c38b102Step 2 — Switch to Target Branch
git switch masteror
git checkout masterStep 3 — Cherry-Pick the Commit
git cherry-pick c38b102Git copies that commit onto the current branch.
Before Cherry-Pick
master
A ----- Bfeature-2
A ----- B ----- C ----- D ----- E
^
Bug Fix
After Cherry-Pick
master
A ----- B ----- D'feature-2
A ----- B ----- C ----- D ----- E
Notice:

• D' is not the original commit.

• It is a new commit containing the same code changes.
Why Does Cherry-Pick Create a New Commit?
Git does not reuse the original commit.
Example
Original commit
Commit ID
8b3f21Cherry-picked commit
Commit ID
c91de8The code is identical, but the commit hash is different.
Why Is the Hash Different?
A Git commit hash depends on:

• Parent commit

• Author

• Committer

• Timestamp

• Commit message

• File contents
Since the parent commit changes, Git generates a new SHA.
Merge vs Cherry-Pick
Merge
master
A ----- B
\
\
feature C ----- D ----- ERun
git merge featureResult
master
A ----- B --------------- M
\ /
C ----- D --- EEverything is merged.
Cherry-Pick
master
A ----- Bfeature
A ----- B ----- C ----- D ----- E
Run
git cherry-pick DResult
master
A ----- B ----- D'Only one commit is copied.
Cherry-Pick Multiple Commits
git cherry-pick commit1 commit2 commit3Example
feature
A --- B --- C --- D --- ERun
git cherry-pick C DResult
master
A --- B --- C' --- D'Cherry-Pick a Range of Commits
git cherry-pick A^..DThis copies:
A
B
C
DThe ^ means "include commit A as well."
Example
feature
A --- B --- C --- D --- EResult
master
A --- B --- C --- DHandling Conflicts
If Git finds conflicts:
Auto-merging app.js
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in app.jsResolve the conflicts manually.
Then continue.
git add .
git cherry-pick --continueIf you want to cancel:
git cherry-pick --abortCommon Cherry-Pick Commands
Cherry-pick one commit
git cherry-pick <commit_hash>Cherry-pick multiple commits
git cherry-pick commit1 commit2 commit3Cherry-pick a range
git cherry-pick A^..DContinue after resolving conflicts
git cherry-pick --continueAbort
git cherry-pick --abortReal-World Example
Suppose you’re building an e-commerce application.
Feature branch contains:
Commit 1 → Shopping Cart
Commit 2 → Payment Gateway
Commit 3 → Login Bug Fix
Commit 4 → WishlistProduction urgently needs the login fix.
Do not merge the whole branch:
git merge featureInstead:
git cherry-pick <login_bug_commit_hash>Only the login bug fix reaches production.
Common Use Cases
Use git cherry-pick when:

• ✅ A bug fix must be moved to another branch.

• ✅ A hotfix needs to be applied to both main and a release branch.

• ✅ Only one specific commit is required.

• ✅ You accidentally committed on the wrong branch.

• ✅ Backporting a fix to an older release branch.
When Not to Use Cherry-Pick
Avoid cherry-pick when:

• ❌ You want the entire feature branch.

• ❌ The commits are tightly coupled and should remain together.

• ❌ You’re regularly synchronizing branches (use merge or rebase instead).
Interview Questions
Q1. What is git cherry-pick?
Answer:
git cherry-pick copies one or more specific commits from one branch to another without merging the entire branch.
Q2. What is the difference between Merge and Cherry-Pick?
| Git Merge | Git Cherry-Pick |
| -------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- |
| Merges an entire branch | Copies selected commit(s) only |
| Preserves complete branch history | Creates new commit(s) |
| Brings all commits | Brings only specified commits |
| Creates a merge commit (unless fast-forward) | Creates a new commit with a different hash |
| Used for integrating completed branches | Used for bug fixes, hotfixes, or selective changes |Q3. Does git cherry-pick preserve the original commit hash?
Answer:
No.
Git creates a new commit with a new commit hash because the parent commit and commit metadata differ, even though the code changes are identical.
Q4. What happens if there is a conflict during cherry-pick?
Answer:

• Resolve the conflicts manually.

• Stage the resolved files:
git add .3. Continue:
git cherry-pick --continueOr abort the operation:
git cherry-pick --abortQuick Cheat Sheet
| Command | Purpose |
| ---------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| `git cherry-pick <hash>` | Copy one commit |
| `git cherry-pick A B C` | Copy multiple commits |
| `git cherry-pick A^..D` | Copy a range of commits |
| `git cherry-pick --continue` | Continue after resolving conflicts |
| `git cherry-pick --abort` | Cancel the cherry-pick |
| `git log --oneline` | Find commit hashes |
| `git switch master` | Switch to target branch |Remember: Use git merge when you want to integrate an entire branch, and use git cherry-pick when you need only specific commit(s), such as urgent bug fixes or hotfixes.
